problem.title: Minimum Size Subarray Sum
problem.description: Given an array of positive integers nums and a positive integer target, return the minimal length of a contiguous subarray [numsl, numsl+1, ..., numsr-1, numsr] of which the sum is greater than or equal to target.

example.explanation: Subarray [4,3] sums to 7 with length 2. Sliding window: expand right, shrink left when sum ≥ target.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Sliding Window [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Expand the window right by adding each element, and whenever the window sum meets or exceeds the target, shrink from the left while recording the window length. Because all values are positive, shrinking always decreases the sum and expanding always increases it, making the greedy two-pointer approach correct. The key insight is that positivity guarantees the window sum is monotone with respect to window size.
